title: Midnight Slovakian Galangal Crumble
description: A sweet, crumbly dessert that blends the zing of galangal with the comforting warmth of a pressurecooked apple filling, finished with a buttery oat crumble.
instructions: Preheat the pressure cooker to the sauté setting and melt the butter in the pot.
Add the apples, galangal, lemon juice, salt, and brown sugar; sauté for 2 minutes until the apples soften slightly.
Stir in the honey and flour, mixing until the mixture thickens, then add the water and close the pressure cooker lid.
Cook on high pressure for 5 minutes, then quick‑release the pressure safely.
While the filling is cooking, combine the rolled oats, remaining brown sugar, and cinnamon in a bowl.
Once the pressure cooking is complete, spoon the apple‑galangal mixture into a baking dish and spread the oat topping evenly over it.
Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C (350°F) for 15 minutes, or until the topping is golden and crisp.
Serve warm, optionally topped with a scoop of vanilla yogurt or a drizzle of honey.
